questions.question: How does the figurative language in paragraph 14 help readers understand Rachel's reaction to the sweater?

responses.content: C. The metaphors in the paragraph make it clear to readers that Rachel is overreacting about the sweater.

questions.question: How does the figurative language in paragraph 19 help readers visualize Rachel's behavior?

responses.content: C. The similes about her body shaking “like when you have the hiccups” and her head hurting “like when you drink milk too fast” connect to unpleasant experiences most readers have had.

questions.question:
Throughout the excerpt, Sal watches the Winterbottom family interact and then reflects on what matters, ‘in the course of a lifetime.’

According to the last paragraph, how does watching the Winterbottom family help Sal understand the difference between things that matter and things that don't matter in life? Use evidence from the text to support your response.

"Oh," she said. "Who is doing this?" She held out the piece of paper: In the course of a lifetime, what does it matter? Prudence said, "Well, I have more important things to worry about, I can assure you.
